lede: A look at March 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Dario Fo and Luigi Einaudi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States
events.desc: Students across the United States stage the March for Our Lives demanding gun control in response to the Stoneman Douglas High School shooting.

events.title: Germanwings Flight 9525
events.desc: Germanwings Flight 9525 crashes in the French Alps in an apparent pilot mass murder-suicide, killing all 150 people on board.

events.title: Bhutan
events.desc: Bhutan officially becomes a democracy, with its first ever general election.

events.title: El Salvador
events.desc: El Salvadorian Archbishop Óscar Romero is assassinated while celebrating Mass in San Salvador.

events.title: Argentina
events.desc: In Argentina, the armed forces overthrow the constitutional government of President Isabel Perón and start a seven-year dictatorial period self-styled the National Reorganization Process.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In an event later dramatized in the movie The Great Escape, 76 Allied prisoners of war begin breaking out of the German camp Stalag Luft III.

events.title: Tydings–McDuffie Act
events.desc: The Tydings–McDuffie Act is passed by the United States Congress, allowing the Philippines to become a self-governing commonwealth.

events.title: 1921 Women's Olympiad
events.desc: The 1921 Women's Olympiad begins in Monte Carlo, becoming the first international women's sports event.

events.title: Robert Koch
events.desc: Robert Koch announces the discovery of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, the bacterium responsible for tuberculosis.

events.title: James VI and I
events.desc: James VI of Scotland is proclaimed King James I of England and Ireland, upon the death of Elizabeth I.

births.name: Dario Fo
births.line: Italian playwright, actor, director, and composer, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Luigi Einaudi
births.line: Italian economist and politician, 2nd president of the Italian Republic

births.name: Adolf Butenandt
births.line: German biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Vojislav Koštunica
births.line: Serbian academic and politician, 8th prime minister of Serbia

births.name: Yanis Varoufakis
births.line: Greek economist and politician, Greek Minister of Finance

deaths.text: Shripad Narayan Pendse, Indian Marathi novelist [citation needed]
Jules Verne, French novelist, poet, and playwright

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mac Cairthinn of Clogher
Christian feast day: Óscar Romero (Catholic Church, Anglican Communion, Lutheranism)
Christian feast day: Paul Couturier (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Walter Hilton (Church of England)
